steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the complementary angle pair
steps.content: Shortcut (sin/cos): In a right triangle, the two acute angles always add to 90°, making them complementary. In right triangle RST, angles R and S satisfy $R + S = 90^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Apply the co-function identity directly
steps.content: When two angles are complementary, $\sin(R) = \cos(S)$. We're given $\sin(R) = \dfrac{2\sqrt{10}}{7}$, so $\cos(S) = \dfrac{2\sqrt{10}}{7}$. That confirms B is correct.
